doctrine: Low-waste, low-noise operation: scoped inputs, transparent operator intent, local-only evidence, no target-side artifacts for survey lanes, and teardown-required payloads for authorized deployment lanes.

lanes.description: Read-only survey and reachability validation from approved manifests.
Local dashboard and relay probes run from the operator machine.
Authorized deployment, mapping, and staging workflows that may mutate targets.

principles.summary: Use approved target manifests, key ports, -silent, -ec, and JSON for parser-facing evidence.
Survey evidence is written only to local ignored paths; target workstations receive no logs, scripts, or agents.
Use Naabu -ec on reachability profiles to avoid wasting probes on CDN/cloud firewall edges.
Use silent machine-readable tool output in pipelines to avoid logos and banner noise.
AD-derived or approved manifests define population; packet probes validate reachability only.
Authorized deployment payloads, scheduled tasks, and transient staging must be removed or explicitly documented as retained.
Legacy deployment/mapping tools are preserved but disabled by default until the operator opts in.
